title: The Scarcity Crisis: Why You Need a Professional Career Counselor
excerpt: India needs 1.5 million career counselors but has fewer than 10,000. Why relying on 'free advice' is costing students their future.
author: MyAarohan Team
content:
We obsess over IIT acceptance rates, but ignore a larger crisis: The Scarcity of Guidance. 93% of Indian schools lack a dedicated counselor. The ratio is 1 counselor for every 3,000 students. This void is filled by unverified internet searches, peer pressure, and the "Uncle Network" giving advice from 1990.

The "Village" Cannot Keep Up

There are now over 12,000 distinct career paths. Does your neighborhood uncle know about Bioinformatics or Prompt Engineering? Relying on them is like asking a heart surgeon to fix a Tesla engine—smart, but wrong expertise.

Amateur vs. Professional

  • Amateur Advice: Based on anecdotes ("My cousin did this"), highly biased towards "Safety," uses intuition.
  • Professional Counselor: Based on Data & Psychometrics, zero bias, uses multi-dimensional assessments.

The Cost of "Free" Advice

Free advice is expensive. It costs 15-20 Lakhs in degrees for the wrong career, 3-4 years of youth wasted, and a massive loss of confidence. A career mentorship session is an insurance policy against a mismatched career.

The job market is a war zone. Don't send your child in without a strategist.
